a:r.a:t: Outsourced Basic Construction (Annual TAM) | After averaging ~$3.5B through FY2025, demand peaks at ~$5.5B in FY2024 and FY2026 and is expected to hold ~$4.3-5.7B a year through FY2031.
Note: Columbia had no hull authorized in FY22/23/25 (hull costs shown in year of authorization)
Lower bound reflects FY22–25 avg. rate; Upper bound assumes penetration grows ~1.8 ppts p.a. for
) targets, driven by “strategically outsourcing workload to qualified suppliers” (PB27 SCN, Columbia P-10)
OBBBA adds $5.4B for two additional DDG-51s in FY26 (Sec. 20002(17)); FYDP calls for ~1.5-ship buys p.a. FY27-FY31

FY26 spike driven by OBBBA-related EOQ AP/LLTM (vendor-purchased LLTM bought ahead of the FY27-31 ramp flows almost entirely to suppliers); FY27–31 lower bound reflects FY22–25 avg. rate (i.e., reversion to mean); FY27-31 upper bound assumes ~1.1 ppts p.a. (~7% CAGR) towards a ~19% target using FY22-25 avg. as baseline, driven by stated intent to grow outsourced manhours ~30%

Sources: Navy SCN P-5c / P-40 budget justification, FY22–FY27, and PB27 FYDP outyears (FY28–FY31); FY26, PL 119-21 Sec. 20002; OUSD(C) Green Book Procurement deflators; Navy Shipbuilding Plan; HII FY24 Q3 / FY25 Q4 / FY26 Q1 earnings calls
